title: 10 Herbal Teas to Help Clear a Runny Nose
intro_desc: Herbal teas for runny nose are natural remedies that help alleviate congestion and nasal discharge by promoting decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The best herbal teas for runny nose include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Sambucus nigra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Rosmarinus officinalis, Echinacea angustifolia, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, runny nose can disrupt daily life, causing fatigue, decreased productivity, and social isolation, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural remedy that can help with a runny nose. This is because eucalyptus oil, which is found in the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, has decongestant properties. When you drink eucalyptus globulus tea, the eucalyptus oil is released into your body and helps to thin and clear out mucus from your nose and sinuses. This can provide quick relief from congestion and a runny nose, making it easier to breathe and feel better. Eucalyptus globulus tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, further helping to alleviate a runny nose. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural expectorant, which means it can help to loosen and bring up mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and clear out.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins, specifically found in lavender oil, are good for runny nose because they have natural decongestant properties that help reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
Quercetin is good for runny nose because it helps reduce inflammation in the nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion caused by allergies or colds.

Eucalyptol is good for runny nose because it acts as a natural decongestant that helps to break down and clear out excess mucus and other irritants from the nasal passages, providing quick relief from congestion and sinus pressure.

Tannins (Lavender oil) are good for runny nose because they have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for runny nose
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with a runny nose, first, you need dried lavender flowers. You can buy them from a health food store or online. Next, boil one cup of water in a teapot. Then, add one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to the hot water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, so the flavors can come out. After that, strain the tea using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the lavender flowers. You can add honey or lemon to the tea if you like it sweeter or more sour. Now, your tea is ready to drink. To help with a runny nose, drink the tea two to three times a day, preferably when you feel the symptoms coming on. This will help to calm your nasal passages and reduce the flow of mucus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea has been used for a long time to help with colds and runny noses. It contains special compounds that boost the immune system, which helps the body fight off infections that can cause a runny nose. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. This makes it easier to breathe and feel better. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea tea also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. When taken as a tea, Echinacea purpurea can help soothe a sore throat and calm a cough, which can be helpful when you have a runny nose. Some people also use it to help prevent illnesses from occurring in the first place, by giving their immune system a boost.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, which makes them good for relieving a runny nose.
Flavonoids are good for runny nose because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Cichoric acid is good for runny nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for runny nose
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with a runny nose, start by gathering dried lavender flowers. Use one teaspoon of dried lavender for every cup of boiling water. Pour the boiling water over the lavender in a teapot or a cup, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the flavor will be. While the tea is steeping, you can add a little honey to sweeten it, if you like. Once the tea has steeped, strain it to remove the lavender flowers and pour it into a cup. You can drink it hot or cold, whichever you prefer. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of lavender tea a day to help ease a runny nose. You can also add a few drops of lavender essential oil to the tea if you have it, but make sure to dilute it with water first. Lavender tea has natural decongestant properties that can help reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it a great natural remedy for a runny nose.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy that can help with a runny nose. The main reason is that ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This makes it easier for you to breathe and feel better. The tea also contains compounds that help to thin out mucus, making it less likely to clog your nose. When you drink ginger tea, the warm liquid helps to loosen up any blockages in your sinuses, allowing you to breathe more easily. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to reduce congestion and sinus pressure, giving you relief from a runny nose.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are not actually present, but rather the compound Gingerols is found in Zingiber officinale (Ginger). Gingerols in Zingiber officinale are good for a runny nose because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
Shogaols are good for runny nose because they have natural decongestant properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.

Polyphenols are good for runny nose because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and calm the body's immune response, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for runny nose
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with a runny nose, you will need a few fresh or dried lavender flowers. Start by boiling a kettle with fresh water. While waiting for the water to boil, measure out a teaspoon of dried lavender flowers or 2-3 fresh flowers for every 8 oz of water. Once the water has reached its boiling point, pour it over the lavender flowers in a teapot or a cup. Allow the mixture to steep for about 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired strength of flavor. After the steeping time has passed, carefully strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or a tea strainer to remove the lavender flowers. You can add honey or lemon to your tea if desired, but be sure to start with a small amount as lavender can be quite potent. Once you have strained the tea, you can drink it hot or cold, depending on your preference. Drinking this tea regularly may help to ease congestion and reduce the frequency of a runny nose due to its natural decongestant properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a herb that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including a runny nose. The reason thyme tea is good for a runny nose is that it has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties. These properties help to fight off infections that cause a runny nose, such as the common cold or flu. Thyme tea also contains compounds that help to thin and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. The essential oils in thyme, particularly thymol, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. When you drink thyme tea, the compounds are absorbed into your body and can help to calm down your nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and runny nose symptoms. Thyme tea is also easy to make and can be enjoyed hot or cold, making it a convenient remedy for a runny nose.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with a runny nose, start by gathering 1 tablespoon of dried lavender flowers and 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves. You will also need a cup of boiling water and a strainer or tea infuser. Begin by placing the dried herbs in the strainer or tea infuser, then pour in the boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled slightly and the flavors have been released. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the herbs. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink the tea while it's still warm, as the heat helps to open up your nasal passages and relieve congestion. Drinking this tea 2-3 times a day can help to soothe a runny nose and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular remedy for a runny nose. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain special chemicals that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. When the nasal passages swell, they produce extra mucus, causing a runny nose. The chemicals in Ginkgo biloba tea calm down the swelling, allowing the nasal passages to return to normal. This helps to reduce the amount of mucus produced, making it easier to breathe and feel better. Ginkgo biloba tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and irritation in the nasal passages. As a result, drinking Ginkgo biloba tea may help to alleviate symptoms of a runny nose, such as congestion and sinus pressure. By reducing inflammation and swelling, Ginkgo biloba tea may also help to prevent sinus infections and other complications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary tea, is a herbal remedy that can help alleviate a runny nose. The tea is made from the leaves of the rosemary plant, which have natural decongestant properties. When you drink rosemary tea, the decongestant compounds can help to open up airways and reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This can make it easier to breathe and can help to stop the flow of mucus, which can be causing your runny nose. Additionally, rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the nasal lining and reduce irritation. As a result, rosemary tea may help to provide relief from congestion and sinus pressure, making it a popular choice for people who suffer from allergies or colds.

Next, boil a cup of water in a kettle or pot and let it cool down for a minute or two. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to strain the tea before drinking to avoid any lavender bits. Once the tea is ready, drink it hot or cold to help soothe your runny nose and ease congestion. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day, especially when you're feeling congested or need to clear your sinuses. Keep in mind that while lavender tea can be helpful, it's not a substitute for medical treatment and you should consult a doctor if your symptoms persist.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe a runny nose. This is because the tea contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink the tea, the glycyrrhizin travels to your nose and reduces swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages. As a result, the production of mucus in your nose decreases, and you experience less congestion and dripping. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce the irritation and redness in the nasal area, making it a good option for people with allergies or sinusitis. The tea may also help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel from your body.

These properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. When you drink the tea, the anti-inflammatory compounds are absorbed into your body and can help to calm down the nasal tissues, reducing the production of excess mucus. This can provide quick relief from runny nose, congestion, and sinus pressure. The tea also has natural decongestant properties, which can help to clear out mucus and other debris that can clog up your sinuses, allowing for easier airflow and breathing. By using Hyptis suaveolens tea, you can naturally soothe your runny nose and start feeling better.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with a runny nose, start by gathering dried lavender flowers. You can purchase these at a local health food store or online. Next, measure out one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers for every cup of boiling water you plan to use. Place the lavender in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag to make it easy to remove the flowers after steeping. Pour boiling water over the lavender and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired strength of tea. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the lavender flowers. You can add honey to taste if you prefer a sweeter tea. Once the tea has cooled slightly, you can drink it as needed to help soothe a runny nose. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day to experience the full benefits of the lavender. Remember to always check with a healthcare professional before trying new herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

supplementary_best: A blend of Eucalyptus globulus, Sambucus nigra, and Thymus vulgaris is effective in relieving a runny nose. Eucalyptus globulus, with its decongestant properties, helps to clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion. Sambucus nigra, rich in antiviral compounds, fights off underlying infections that may be causing the runny nose. Thymus vulgaris, a natural antibacterial agent, prevents bacterial growth in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and further congestion. This combination provides a well-rounded approach to addressing the root causes of a runny nose.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a runny nose, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can make it worse. Peppermint tea (Mentha piperita) is one to avoid as it can increase mucus production and make congestion worse. Another herb to steer clear of is Yerba Mate (Ilex paraguariensis), as it can cause stomach upset, which can lead to more congestion. You should also limit or avoid Sage (Salvia officinalis) tea, as it can dry out your mucus membranes and worsen a runny nose. Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis) tea is also not recommended as it can cause an increase in mucus production. Additionally, avoid teas made from Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) as it can cause stomach upset and worsen respiratory issues, including a runny nose.
supplementary_other_remedies: Eucalyptus oil can help ease a runny nose by loosening mucus and opening airways. It's often added to a warm bath or applied to the chest to ease congestion. This is because eucalyptus oil contains compounds that reduce inflammation and make it easier to breathe. Nasal saline irrigation can also be helpful, as it involves flushing out mucus and debris from the nasal passages with a saltwater solution. This helps to moisturize the nasal lining, reduce swelling, and clear out excess mucus. Some people also find that inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water or a steam humidifier helps to ease congestion and loosen mucus. This is because the heat and moisture can help to break down and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe.
supplementary_related_ailments: If you have a runny nose, you may also experience sinus pressure, congestion, and headaches. This is because the nasal passages and sinuses are connected, so when one area gets irritated, the other areas can get affected too. Some medicinal herbs that can help alleviate these symptoms are:  - Echinacea: This herb is known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help fight off the infection causing the runny nose. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease congestion. - Ginger: Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce sinus pressure and ease congestion. It can also help loosen mucus and make it easier to breathe. - Peppermint: Peppermint oil has a cooling effect that can help reduce nasal congestion and ease sinus pressure. It also has antibacterial properties that can help fight off infections. - Eucalyptus: Eucalyptus oil has decongestant properties that can help open up airways and ease congestion. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections. - Thyme: Thyme has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections causing the runny nose. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease congestion.  These herbs can be used in various forms, such as teas, oils, or supplements, to help alleviate the symptoms of a runny nose. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any new remedies, especially if you have a pre-existing condition or are taking any medications.
